data.title: Italy's Meloni picks new tourism minister in shake-up after referendum
data.description:
Italy's culture undersecretary Gianmarco Mazzi was sworn in on Friday as the country's new tourism minister, the latest change in Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni's government after her defeat in a referendum on judicial reform.

Former television and entertainment executive Mazzi replaces Daniela Santanche, who is facing trial over accounting charges linked to a publishing group she previously owned and resigned from her ministry post last week under pressure from Meloni.

Meloni wrote on her X social media account that Mazzi's background in the culture and entertainment sector would be an asset for Italy, posting a photograph of the swearing-in ceremony with President Sergio Mattarella.

Since last week's referendum defeat, Meloni has sought to reassert control by removing scandal-plagued figures from her government. Two senior justice ministry officials have also stepped down.

data.title: Russia's national wealth fund liquid assets down to $47.8 bln as of April 1
data.description:
Liquid assets in Russia's national wealth fund (NWF) stood at $47.8 billion as of April 1, or 1.7% of projected gross domestic product for 2026, down from $51.8 billion a month earlier, the Finance Ministry said on Friday.

Total assets in the wealth fund, which include investments in domestic infrastructure projects, fell to the equivalent of $165 billion or 5.7% of GDP, from $175.3 billion at the start of March, the ministry said.

In March, the ministry spent 59.7 billion roubles ($747.18 million) from the NWF to cover the budget deficit, bringing the total amount to almost 460 billion roubles as of the end of the first quarter.

($1 = 79.9000 roubles)

data.title: Braemar Extends Advisory Agreement With Ashford for 10 Years Through 2037
data.description:
Braemar Hotels & Resorts received an election from its external advisor to extend their advisory agreement for an additional ten years. The extended term will begin on January 24, 2027 and run through January 24, 2037, with all existing terms remaining in effect. The parties retain rights under the agreement to renegotiate the Base Fee or Incentive Fee during the extended term. The move secures long-term continuity of management advisory services.

Agreement details:

  • Agreement type: Advisory agreement extension (management services)
  • Counterparty: Ashford; Ashford Hospitality Advisors
  • Signed / Effective: Mar 31 2026 / Jan 24 2027
  • Duration / Termination: 10 years (Jan 24 2027 to Jan 24 2037)
  • Reason: Maintain long-term external advisory relationship

Original SEC Filing: Braemar Hotels & Resorts Inc. [ BHR ] - 8-K - Apr. 03, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Bitcoin Looks 'Weak.' Jobs Report, Iran War Could Change Things for Struggling Cryptos. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Jack Denton

Bitcoin and other cryptocurrencies were little changed on Friday, with the outlook for the leading digital asset appearing technically weak. Traders should not underestimate what a long weekend could bring.

The price of Bitcoin was up less than 1% over the past 24 hours, hovering below $67,000. The largest digital asset by market capitalization, like the S&P 500 and most stocks, has languished in risk-averse trading since the start of the Iran war on Feb. 28. That has added to declines as Bitcoin has lost more than a third of its value since late-2025 highs comfortably above $100,000.

"Bitcoin has broken the floor of the rising trend channel in the short term, which indicates a weaker initial rising rate. The currency has broken down through support," according to technical analysis from market research group Investtech. "This predicts a further decline...The currency is overall assessed as technically slightly negative for the short term."

Nevertheless, Bitcoin could stand out over the long weekend as cryptos trade constantly while the stock market is closed on Friday.

Digital assets have recently seemed to be a waning indicator of risk sentiment. But an extra day when little else is trading — at a time when breaking news from the Middle East is whipsawing markets — is a chance for Bitcoin to shine. The jobs report for March could also be a macroeconomic catalyst that leads investors to trade Bitcoin due to a lack of anything else.

Beyond Bitcoin, Ethereum — the second-largest crypto — rose less than 1% and was above $2,000. Smaller cryptos, or altcoins, were performing similarly, with Ripple and Solana less than 1% higher and Dogecoin just above 1% in the green.

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Infrastrutture Wireless Italiane Earnings Up In FY25; Guides FY26
data.description:
ROME (dpa-AFX) - Infrastrutture Wireless Italiane S.p.A. (INW.MI), on Friday, reported higher full-year 2025 net income compared with the previous year, proposed a dividend, and outlined its 2026 outlook.

For the full year 2025, net income increased to 360.8 million euros from 353.8 million euros in the previous year.

EBIT rose to 578.3 million euros from 558.9 million euros in the same period last year.

EBITDA jumped to 984.4 million euros from 946.7 million euros in the prior year.

EBITDA margin maintained at 91.4 percent compared with the previous year.

Revenue increased to 1.08 billion euros from 1.04 billion euros in the previous year.

Further, the board has proposed a dividend of 0.5543 euros per share for the 2025 financial year, up 7.5% from the previous year.

The dividend will be paid on May 20, with an ex-dividend date of May 18 and a record date of May 19, and is in line with the company's dividend policy.

The company expects a dividend per share of at least 0.55 euros over the medium term.

Looking ahead, the company expects 2026 revenue in the range of 1.05 billion euros to 1.09 billion euros, with an EBITDA margin of around 90%.

The company also expects dividend per share to be at least in line with 2025 at 0.55 euros and financial leverage of about 5.5x.

On Thursday, Infrastrutture Wireless Italiane closed trading 2.49% higher at EUR 7.19 on the Milan Stock Exchange.

data.title: Lindsay Corporation Announces Quarterly Cash Dividend
data.description:
OMAHA, Neb.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--April 03, 2026--

Lindsay Corporation , a leading global manufacturer and distributor of irrigation and infrastructure equipment and technology, announced today that its Board of Directors has declared a regular quarterly cash dividend of $0.37 per share, payable May 29, 2026, to shareholders of record at the close of business on May 15, 2026.

As of April 1, 2026, Lindsay Corporation had approximately 10.4 million shares outstanding, which are traded on the New York Stock Exchange under the symbol LNN.

About the Company

Lindsay Corporation is a leading global manufacturer and distributor of irrigation and infrastructure equipment and technology. Established in 1955, the company has been at the forefront of research and development of innovative solutions to meet the food, fuel, fiber and transportation needs of the world's rapidly growing population. The Lindsay family of irrigation brands includes Zimmatic(TM) center pivot and lateral move agricultural irrigation systems, FieldNET(TM) and FieldWise(TM) remote irrigation management technology, FieldNET Advisor(TM) irrigation scheduling technology, and industrial IoT solutions. Also a global leader in the transportation industry, Lindsay Transportation Solutions manufactures equipment to improve road safety and keep traffic moving on the world's roads, bridges and tunnels, through the Road Zipper(TM) and Snoline(TM) brands. For more information about Lindsay Corporation, visit http://www.lindsay.com/.

Concerning Forward-Looking Statements

This release contains forward-looking statements that are subject to risks and uncertainties and which reflect management's current beliefs and estimates of future economic circumstances, industry conditions, Company performance and financial results. You can find a discussion of many of these risks and uncertainties in the annual, quarterly and current reports that the Company files with the Securities and Exchange Commission. Forward-looking statements include information concerning possible or assumed future results of operations and planned financing of the Company and those statements preceded by, followed by or including the words "anticipate," "estimate," "believe," "intend," "expect," "outlook," "could," "may," "should," "will," or similar expressions. For these statements, the Company claims the protection of the safe harbor for forward-looking statements contained in the Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995. The Company undertakes no obligation to update any forward-looking information contained in this press release.

data.title: Shanghai warehouse aluminium stocks approach six-year high
data.description:
Aluminium stocks in warehouses monitored by the Shanghai Futures Exchange approached a six-year high on Friday, as dramatic price volatility stirred by supply disruptions due to the Iran war dampened buying appetite.

  • Aluminium inventories in the Shanghai exchange-approved warehouses (AL-STX-SGH) jumped on Friday by 3.4% from the week before to 470,108 tons, the highest since April 17, 2020.

  • The weekly rise marked a pick-up in stocks for a 15th consecutive session.

  • "Aluminium fabricators, whose revenue mainly comes from processing fees, are restocking on a hand-to-mouth basis in the face of sharp price volatility," said Liangmin Zhu, an analyst at consultancy Aladdiny.

  • High stocks prevented domestic prices from rising on a similar scale to international ones.

  • Aluminium prices on the London Metal Exchange have jumped by 10% since the Iran war, versus a rise of 3.5% on the Shanghai Futures Exchange .

  • The war has frozen shipments amid the effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz, sparking fears of reduced supply while Iran's attacks on two of the region's biggest aluminium smelters have heightened the supply fear.

  • A drawdown in inventories is likely to be seen in mid to late April, helped by improving home demand, said analysts.

  • "A stabilisation in prices could help to stimulate downstream demand," said Aladdiny's Zhu, adding that better exports may also help destock.

  • China's aluminium exports now poised to grow, with some earlier forecasts for flat shipments revised sharply higher.

data.title: Are Oil Markets Open on Good Friday? U.N. Delays Vote on Opening Strait of Hormuz. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Adam Clark

Oil traders are watching developments in the Iran conflict over the long weekend as the United Nations Security Council has postponed a vote on protecting shipping in the Strait of Hormuz.

But commodity markets are largely closed for the Good Friday holiday so oil contracts aren't trading. Eyes are on how much traffic makes it through the Strait of Hormuz — which normally carries around 20% of the world's oil trade — for clues as to where crude prices go from here.

Brent crude, the international standard, settled at $109.24 a barrel late on Thursday, while West Texas Intermediate, the U.S. standard, settled at $112.06 a barrel.

The rare situation where WTI prices appear higher than Brent is due to a discrepancy in contract timing, with the WTI market still trading the May contract as the soonest delivery month, while the Brent market has already rolled over to June. The WTI June contract settled at $97.75 a barrel.

The U.N. Security Council was set to vote Friday morning on a draft resolution "to use all defensive means necessary and commensurate with the circumstances," to secure transit passage through the strait for at least six months, but the vote appears to have been postponed, with no new date announced.

President Donald Trump has urged other countries to launch their own operation to wrest control of the strait from Iran. French President Emmanuel Macron and South Korean President Lee Jae-myung on Friday said their countries would work together to stabilize the situation in the crucial waterway, in a joint press conference during a summit in Seoul, but did not elaborate on how they would do so, according to the Associated Press.

A French-owned container ship has transited through the Strait of Hormuz, the first time a vessel with ties to Western Europe has been known to traverse the channel since Iran began blocking it at the start of the war, The Wall Street Journal reported.

However, fighting is continuing in the Middle East. Kuwait Petroleum, the country's national oil company, said its Mina Al-Ahmadi refinery was targeted in a drone attack, triggering fires, according to Kuwait state media.

Write to Adam Clark at adam.clark@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: XRP price prediction: What next for the falling Ripple token?
data.description:
XRP price has pulled back in the past few months and constantly remains in a technical bear market as demand from investors continues falling. Ripple token was trading at $1.3115 on Friday, down by over 60% from its all-time high of $3.6. This consolidation continued despite the major progress that Ripple Labs is making.

Ripple Labs launches Treasury system for fiat and crypto assets 

XRP price has remained in a bear market even after Ripple Labs launched a treasury system that unifies fiat and crypto assets, especially Ripple USD (RLUSD). The solution is connected to over 13,000 global banks alongside on-chain assets.

It is part of Ripple’s strategy to integrate G-Treasury, which it acquired last year in a $1.2 billion deal. G-Treasury was later on rebranded to Ripple Prime, and is used to manage the corporate treasury of top companies like Rubix, Volvo Cars, Good Year, and Canadian Tire. https://twitter.com/bgarlinghouse/status/2039507762145604061

In another announcement, the company partnered with Convera, a top player in commercial payments. The partnership will enable Convera to offer crypto-enabled payment and treasury solutions for businesses. It will enable faster and more reliable cross-border payments. In a statement, Aaron Slettehaugh, a senior executive at Ripple said:

“By partnering with Convera, we’re combining a trusted global payment infrastructure with stablecoin-powered settlement to give businesses more control over how and when they move value across borders.”

XRP demand is fading

XRP price has stagnated despite these good news because of its fading demand among investors. 

Data shows that demand for spot XRP ETFs has waned, with their outflows in March rising to over $30 million. The funds have already shed over $1.2 million this month, bringing the cumulative total net inflows to $1.21 billion. All these funds now hold over $916 million in assets.

The same trend is happening in the spot market, where the daily volume dropped to $1.9 billion. In the past, it was common for the daily volume of the XRP token to be over $10 billion.

The futures open interest has also plunged in the past few months. Data shows that the futures open interest has tumbled to $2.4 billion from last year's high of over $11 billion. The open interest continued its strong downward trend after October 10 when liquidations surged to over $20 billion.

XRP price prediction: Technical analysis 

Ripple price chart | Source: TradingView 

The daily timeframe chart shows that the XRP price has slumped in the past few months, falling from a high of $3.6 in July last year to $1.31 today. It formed a death cross pattern on March 2nd as the 50-day and 200-day Exponential Moving Averages (EMA) crossed each other.

The coin is now forming a bearish flag pattern, which is made up of a vertical line and a horizontal channel. It also remains below the Supertrend indicator and the Ichimoku cloud.

Therefore, for most likely XRP price forecast is bearish, with the next key target being at $1, which is about 23% below the current level.

data.title: Palm closes at 15-month high, logs fifth straight weekly gain
data.description:
Malaysian palm oil futures edged almost 1% higher on Friday to log a fifth straight weekly gain, driven by expectations of lower inventories, Indonesia's B50 biodiesel plans and heightened Middle East tensions.

The benchmark palm oil contract for June delivery on the Bursa Malaysia Derivatives Exchange gained 47 ringgit, or 0.98%, to 4,838 ringgit ($1,201.09) a metric ton, the highest closing since December 13, 2024. The contract gained 4.47% this week.

Lower March-end stocks in Malaysia, the possibility of Indonesia implementing its B50 biodiesel mandate, highly volatile crude oil prices and uncertainty surrounding U.S. President Donald Trump's policies have kept prices elevated, said Sandeep Singh, director of The Farm Trade, a Kuala Lumpur-based consulting and trading company.

Some buyers have also begun to re-enter the market after a prolonged wait, although prices above 4,900 ringgit are seen as too expensive, he added.

Malaysia’s palm oil inventories likely dropped in March by the most in three years to their lowest level since last July, as a surge in exports more than offset a modest increase in output, a Reuters survey showed.

The Malaysian Palm Oil Board (MPOB) is scheduled to release its March supply and demand data on April 10.

Oil prices surged overnight, with U.S. crude settling more than 11% higher and Brent jumping nearly 8% on Thursday in volatile trade. Oil markets are closed on Friday for a public holiday.

Dalian's most-active soyoil contract (DBYcv1) rose 0.53%, while its palm oil contract gained 0.59%. The Chicago Board of Trade was closed for a public holiday.

Palm oil tracks the price movements of rival edible oils as it competes for a share of the global vegetable oils market.

The ringgit , palm's currency of trade, firmed 0.2% against the dollar, making the commodity more expensive for buyers holding foreign currencies.

India's palm oil imports fell nearly 19% to a three-month low in March, as a rally in tropical oil prices, tracking energy markets, prompted refiners to curb purchases while awaiting a correction, five dealers said.

($1 = 4.0280 ringgit)

data.title: The 10-Point: The Wall Street Journal's Guide to the Day's Top News
data.description:
By Emma Tucker

After months on the ropes, Pam Bondi thought she was finally safe. She was wrong. "I think it's time," President Trump told the attorney general yesterday, she recounted to an associate. The president had regularly expressed dissatisfaction with the speed at which Bondi handled his agenda, and she had often seemed to go out of her way to appease him. It was never enough.

Today's Headlines

Iran is responding to the threat of a ground invasion by stepping up its defenses and recruiting children, while threatening to attack more targets around the Gulf. Follow live coverage.

Whether the war ends in success or defeat for Iran depends on whether it emerges from the conflict holding the Strait of Hormuz, writes Yaroslav Trofimov.

Harvard's faculty is set to vote next week on a proposal to cap the number of A's per course, which has prompted an outcry from undergraduates.

Sleepy Hartford, Conn., is the hottest housing market in America.

OpenAI's surprise purchase of TBPN, a popular online tech-news show, underscores its efforts to help shape the narrative about AI.

From The Markets

The wealthy investors who powered private credit are now rushing for the exits.

The U.S. economy entered the Iran war on solid footing, and now it is absorbing a global oil shock far better than most of the world — but it isn't fully insulated from the conflict.

Read It Here First

This is the inside story of Pam Bondi's ouster.

For the attorney general, yesterday was as awkward and chaotic as the rest of her 14-month tenure, report Josh Dawsey, Alex Leary, C. Ryan Barber and Sadie Gurman. Bondi and Trump, who decided earlier this week he was replacing her, briefly sat near each other during the Supreme Court's birthright citizenship hearing, but the president soon switched chairs. Later, Bondi would ask Trump if she could keep her job until the summer. He declined. Some people close to Trump and Bondi say it wasn't a single issue that led to her dismissal, but a steady drip of frustrations. Trump hasn't decided on a replacement, White House officials say.

These five moments defined Bondi's tumultuous tenure.

This college student — and his cat meme — hunted the world's biggest cyberweapon.

Benjamin Brundage, a senior at the Rochester Institute of Technology, conducted his own investigation into a network of hacked devices that was launching the biggest cyberattacks ever seen. Investigators didn't know who had built this powerful cyberweapon, or how. Brundage started messaging online with an anonymous user who seemed to have insider knowledge, and got key information after sending a cat meme to lighten the mood. Brundage joined a team that learned that a new internet vulnerability threatened tens of millions of consumers and as much as a quarter of the world's corporations, reports Robert McMillan. His findings impressed veteran researchers-including federal law enforcement, which took action against the network two weeks ago.

Expert Take

Q: Some heirs are saying thanks but no thanks to inheritances. Why?

Reporter Laura Saunders wrote about a useful tax-planning tool that some older savers may want to consider.

A: It's called a disclaimer. In essence, it is a strategic rejection of an inherited asset by an heir. The result is that part or all of the inheritance passes to someone else, often a child or grandchild. The move can save taxes and accomplish other goals for families. It can be used with IRAs and also other inherited assets.

I'm betting many older savers are overlooking disclaimers as an IRA tax-planning tool. I was one of them. However, I recently disclaimed part of a traditional IRA I inherited, and it worked out well. My adult children received a legacy from a relative they loved and the overall taxes will be lower.

As a result, I'm struck by how useful disclaimers can be for retirees and pre-retirees who have traditional IRAs far larger than they expected. Thanks to diligent saving, 401(k) rollovers, and market growth, these accounts are often a retiree's largest asset. While this is a great problem to have, it poses dilemmas for savers trying to minimize income taxes.

See The Story

Homeowners are ditching all-white interiors for something less vanilla.

For years, white walls and light-oak floors have ruled the home-decor world, especially when it's time to sell. But maximalist interiors are gaining traction across the country as homeowners experiment with bold prints and patterns, enveloping colors and showstopper embellishments. Yet opinions are divided on how these types of designs fare when it comes to attracting a buyer.

Happening Today

Stock markets are closed for Good Friday. Bonds will close early.

The Trump administration is expected to submit its annual budget request, for the fiscal year beginning Oct. 1.

Jobs data for March will provide a snapshot of the health of the labor market amid spiking energy prices due to war in the Middle East. Here's what we're watching.

Number Of The Day:

$1.8 trillion

The value of sovereign wealth funds in the U.A.E., making the small country the leading sovereign wealth power in the Gulf. See WSJ's guide to the four wealthiest countries in the region, their funds and the key people to know.

And Finally...

Drivers are celebrating the demise of the stop-start.

Designed to lower emissions by temporarily shutting off the engine while the brake is engaged, the feature also makes driving feel unnatural and jerky, critics argue. After federal incentives during the Obama administration kicked U.S. adoption into high gear, stop-start haters this year finally found their champion: Trump. EPA Administrator Lee Zeldin announced the agency was eliminating the federal credits that contributed to stop-start's spread.

data.title: Mine Worker Dies at Alpha Metallurgical Resources Site
data.description:
By Connor Hart

Alpha Metallurgical Resources said a worker has died after being struck by a piece of rock at its Horse Creek Eagle Mine in West Virginia.

Aaron Warrix, a 53-year-old shuttle car operator, had four-and-a-half years of experience with the company, Alpha said late Thursday.

"We are heartbroken to learn of Aaron's passing," Chief Executive Andy Eidson said. "Our hearts are with Aaron's wife, family and friends."

Alpha said it is working alongside federal and state agencies to complete an investigation into the accident and its circumstances.

Write to Connor Hart at connor.hart@wsj.com

data.title: VIDA Global, Cloud Platform for Deploying and Managing AI Business Agents, Files for NYSE American IPO
data.description:
VIDA Global, a Texas-based software company building an AI Agent Operating System, filed for an initial public offering on the NYSE American under the ticker “VIDA.” The company plans to offer 3,333,334 shares at an estimated price range of $4.50 to $5.00 per share, with a midpoint of $4.75. The Benchmark Company is acting as the representative of the underwriters, and the listing will proceed only if the NYSE American approves the application.

Business Description

VIDA Global is building the AI Agent Operating System for businesses. Its cloud platform is designed to help enterprises, service providers, and software vendors deploy, govern, and manage production-grade AI agents across voice, SMS, email, and web chat. These agents plug into the systems companies already use—such as phones, CRMs, schedulers, ticketing, and billing—so they can perform real work like answering calls, qualifying leads, booking and confirming appointments, triaging support tickets, updating records, and initiating payments.

The company pursues a channel-first go-to-market, enabling managed service providers, telecom advisors, UCaaS/CCaaS platforms, and SaaS vendors to embed and resell VIDA-powered agents with white-label branding, multi-tenant administration, and pass-through billing. Under the hood, the platform brings together omnichannel I/O, multi-LLM orchestration, policy and compliance controls, observability and auto-QA, and usage-based pricing aligned to minutes, messages, and completed actions. VIDA positions itself as the neutral operating layer for agentic workloads, focusing on reliability, compliance, and integrations rather than any single underlying AI model.

Market Overview

  • Total addressable market: U.S. call and contact center outsourcing estimated at ~$22.2B in 2026, growing to ~$29.1B by 2030; base-case AI-enabled spend pool estimated at ~$19.1B in 2026 rising to ~$28.8B by 2030.
  • Market growth: Base-case AI-enabled spend pool CAGR of ~10.8% (2026–2030); globally, call/contact center outsourcing projected to reach ~$163.9B by 2030 (9.8% CAGR).
  • Market position: Channel-led platform embedding AI agents into existing systems of record with multi-tenant tooling and compliance controls.
  • Key competitors: UCaaS/CCaaS suites with AI add-ons, no-code agent builders, developer-first APIs, white-label voice agent platforms, and in-house builds.
  • Industry trends: Shift from seat-based software to agentic, outcome-aligned systems; growing GenAI adoption; emphasis on omnichannel communications and telecom compliance (e.g., STIR/SHAKEN, The Campaign Registry).

Operational Metrics

  • Customers: Two commercial customers accounted for ~29.7% and ~13.4% of 2025 revenue, respectively.
  • Locations: Headquarters in Cedar Park, Texas; distributed operations.
  • Geographic presence: Primarily U.S.-based customers and partners.
  • Partnerships: Channel-led strategy targeting MSPs, telecom advisors, UCaaS/CCaaS platforms, and vertical/horizontal SaaS vendors.
  • Other key metrics: Bitcoin treasury holdings of 11.690 BTC with a fair value of $1,023,306 as of December 31, 2025.

Financials Highlights

  • Revenue (current): $551,383 (2025)
  • Revenue growth: Up 3,729% year-over-year (from $14,402 in 2024)
  • Operating income: Operating loss of $(2,876,102) (2025)
  • Net income: Net loss of $(2,897,803) (2025)

Management

  • Lyle Pratt, Chief Executive Officer and Director - Telecom entrepreneur; founded BetterVoice (acquired by Inteliquent/Sinch); strategy and product leadership in communications.
  • Brandon Robinson, Chief Operating Officer - Nearly two decades in telecom engineering and product leadership; previously VP roles at Inteliquent/Sinch.
  • Timothy Noah Hayes, Chief Product Officer and Director - Co-founded Radius Intelligence; product leader in enterprise SaaS and AI; former EIR at Google’s Gradient Ventures.
  • Mark Lilien, Chief Technology Officer - Co-founded Textline; led engineering and platform scaling across SaaS products.
  • Jordan Gadapee, Chief Marketing Officer - Former CPO/Brand Officer at Textline and CPO at Inunity; led HIPAA-compliant SMS initiatives and brand-led growth.

IPO Structure

  • Issuer: VIDA Global Inc.
  • Filing date: April 02, 2026
  • Proposed ticker: VIDA
  • Exchange: NYSE American
  • Price range: $4.50–$5.00 per share
  • Offering size: Approximately $15.8 million gross (at midpoint), net proceeds estimated at ~$13.3 million
  • Shares offered: 3,333,334 shares (plus 500,000 share over-allotment option)
  • Lead underwriters: The Benchmark Company (lead)
  • Use of proceeds: General corporate purposes, including working capital and operating expenses; potential acquisitions or investments (no binding agreements).

Original SEC Filing: VIDA Global Inc. - S-1 - Apr. 02, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Palm Oil Ends Higher Amid Strong Demand — Market Talk
data.description: Palm oil ended higher, with the Bursa Malaysia Derivatives contract for June delivery up 47 ringgit at 4,838 ringgit a ton. Prices are supported by strong demand, said David Ng, a trader at Kuala Lumpur-based Iceberg X. Inventory levels in the coming weeks are likely to be lower, which also provides support for palm oil prices, he said. Ng sees support for palm oil at 4,800 ringgit a ton and resistance at 4,980 ringgit a ton. (tracy.qu@wsj.com)

data.title: Banc of California Announces Schedule of First Quarter 2026 Earnings Release and Conference Call
data.description:
LOS ANGELES--(BUSINESS WIRE)--April 03, 2026--

Banc of California, Inc. (the "Company") today announced it will release financial results for the first quarter ended March 31, 2026 after market close on Wednesday, April 22, 2026. The Company will host a conference call to discuss these financial results the following day on Thursday, April 23, 2026, at 10:00 a.m. Pacific Time (PT).

Interested parties are welcome to attend the conference call by dialing (888) 317-6003 and referencing event code 5670833. A link to the live audio webcast and the slide presentation for the call will be available on the Company's investor relations website prior to the call. An audio archive of the conference call will be available on the Company's investor relations website within 24 hours after the end of the call.

About Banc of California, Inc.

Banc of California, Inc. is a bank holding company with over $34 billion in assets and the parent company of Banc of California. Banc of California is one of the nation's premier relationship-based business banks, providing banking and treasury management services to small-, middle-market, and venture-backed businesses. Banc of California is the largest independent bank headquartered in Los Angeles and the third largest bank headquartered in California and offers a broad range of loan and deposit products and services through 79 full-service branches located throughout California and in Denver, Colorado, and Durham, North Carolina, as well as through regional offices nationwide. The bank also provides full-service payment processing solutions to its clients and serves the Community Association Management industry nationwide with its technology-forward platform, SmartStreet (TM) . The bank is committed to its local communities through the Banc of California Charitable Foundation, and by supporting organizations that provide financial literacy and job training, small business support, affordable housing, and more. Member FDIC. For more information, please visit us at www.bancofcal.com.

data.title: Yomiuri: Politicians' Awareness Key to Strengthening Japan's Intelligence Capabilities, LDP Official Says
data.description:
By Soichiro Kuboniwa

Yomiuri Shimbun Staff Writer

Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi's administration is pushing for stronger national intelligence capabilities. Although reform efforts in this area have stagnated for about 20 years since the first administration of late Prime Minister Shinzo Abe, the ruling Liberal Democratic Party's Headquarters for Intelligence Strategy in February compiled a proposal for the government to strengthen the nation's intelligence capabilities.

Why is the administration making these moves? Akihisa Shiozaki, an LDP House of Representatives lawmaker who serves as the head of the secretariat of the party panel, discussed the matter during an interview with The Yomiuri Shimbun.

He also highlighted the importance of raising awareness of the issue among politicians. The following is excerpted from the interview.

Growing importance for national security

The Yomiuri Shimbun: Why is the discussion on strengthening intelligence capabilities being pushed for under the Takaichi administration?

Akihisa Shiozaki: Strengthening Japan's intelligence capabilities has been a topic of discussion for nearly 20 years.

From 2006 to 2007, during the first Abe administration, my father, Yasuhisa (Shiozaki), served as chief cabinet secretary. Serving as his secretary, I was involved in a project within the Prime Minister's Office aimed at strengthening the nation's intelligence capabilities.

It is not that the current discussion on strengthening intelligence capabilities arose suddenly. It is part of a broader effort to address security issues, and steps were taken also for (the establishment of) the Law on the Protection of Specially Designated Secrets; the security clearance system that certifies eligibility to handle critical information in the field of economic security; and legislation for enabling active cyber defense.

Momentum grew as Prime Minister Takaichi and LDP Policy Research Council Chairperson Takayuki Kobayashi each expressed their intention to strengthen intelligence capabilities when they ran in the party leadership election in 2025.

Yomiuri: How has the situation changed compared to what it was during the first Abe administration?

Shiozaki: Changes in the security environment surrounding East Asia are a major factor. Concerns are also growing regarding influence operations and interference in electoral processes by foreign governments (and entities).

It also matters that the role of intelligence capabilities has dramatically increased in every security decision-making process with the development of diverse methods — including SIGINT (signals intelligence), which involves intercepting and analyzing communications to gather information.

The LDP's proposal outlined 14 specific measures with three main pillars: raising awareness among politicians; eliminating silos among government bodies; and fundamentally strengthening intelligence-gathering capabilities.

Reviewing counterintelligence protocols

Yomiuri: Why did you include "raising awareness among politicians, who are the 'customers' of intelligence" at the very beginning of the proposal?

Shiozaki: Members of the Diet are expected to safeguard information that intelligence service members have risked their lives to gather. They cannot adopt a passive attitude, like ordering "omakase" (chef's course) at a sushi counter.

Unless politicians first change their mindset regarding the importance and precariousness of intelligence, information will not be utilized effectively, no matter how much the intelligence community is strengthened.

In this context, reviewing counterintelligence protocols to protect critical information from foreign intelligence-gathering activities is crucial for raising awareness.

We have received feedback a number of times from the intelligence communities of like-minded nations that there is room for improvement in Japan's awareness and practices regarding information security from an international perspective.

Japanese lawmakers and bureaucrats are free to bring mobile phones and other devices even to sensitive meetings. It is also problematic, in terms of the risks of eavesdropping and security breaches, that electronic devices can be freely brought into the Prime Minister's Office.

The approach taken by the Five Eyes countries (an intelligence alliance of the United States, the United Kingdom, Canada, Australia and New Zealand) serves as a useful reference. Their protocols are established with a thorough understanding of the extent to which information can be stolen through SIGINT.

If Japan were to regulate the bringing of electronic devices into government facilities — for example, by installing metal detectors at entrances to classified areas of government facilities — it would serve as a catalyst for raising awareness.

Moving away from verbal communication

Yomiuri: How can silos among government ministries and agencies be eliminated?

Shiozaki: We included the documentation of intelligence reports in our proposal. This may have come as a shock to those on the front lines of domestic intelligence.

Japan has long had a culture of relying on verbal communication, and there has been a tendency that the higher classification levels are more likely to not be documented. But the question is whether this is the correct approach for intelligence. Since it is not recorded, it cannot be examined before or after the fact.

As for why interorganizational information sharing is not taking place as expected, it can be attributed to personnel (arrangements). But we also heard concerns about how shared information will be used. If it is properly documented in reports, one can understand where and how their information was used or not used.

From the standpoints of trust, quality assurance and interagency coordination, information should, in principle, be documented. Global standard practices should become the norm in Japan as well.

Yomiuri: Is there an inclination toward the Five Eyes alliance, which focuses on communications interception and the sharing of classified information?

Shiozaki: As nations with advanced intelligence capabilities, there is much we can learn from them. In discussions toward the establishment of the Law on the Protection of Specially Designated Secrets, the U.S. military pointed out concerns regarding the potential for information leaks from Japan, and this became a key factor in the legislation.

We are not in discussions with the aim of joining the Five Eyes, but I believe possessing intelligence capabilities that meet international standards is vital in terms of coordinating with like-minded nations through information sharing.

We have studied many countries, but the U.K. and Australia were particularly instructive. The U.K. is, of course, a historic intelligence powerhouse, and since it also has a parliamentary system like Japan, there are many aspects of its institutional design that can serve as a reference.

Also, although it is not widely known, Australia is an advanced intelligence nation that combines a strict intelligence security system with strong external intelligence capabilities. Australia has an intelligence facility called "Pine Gap," which it operates jointly with the U.S., and conducts analysis of cutting-edge SIGINT and reconnaissance satellite data.

In terms of international standards, while the ability to acquire intelligence is important, the ability to prevent it from being stolen is even more critical.

If Japan's security is lax, the intelligence shared by like-minded nations will leak. Intelligence systems are targeted at their most vulnerable "weak link." If Japan is perceived as weak, those nations will not share intelligence with us.

Taking measures against information leaks

Yomiuri: Japan is increasingly dependent on foreign-made communication equipment at home.

Shiozaki: Regarding communications environment security, it is not realistic to eliminate all risks by having everything be domestically produced.

Given the current global dispersion of supply chains, it is difficult to completely eliminate components originating from specific countries from equipment in circulation.

That is precisely why it is important to take measures to address risks rather than applying a blanket ban.

Especially when handling sensitive information, we must implement technical and operational measures based on the assumption information leaks will occur. To that end, raising understanding and awareness of intelligence is of the utmost importance.

Yomiuri: How will discussions regarding the strengthening of intelligence capabilities proceed within the government and the ruling coalition going forward?

Shiozaki: The party proposal is the result of a series of hearings from various ministries and other organs after the party leadership election last autumn.

Even the ministries and agencies responsible for domestic intelligence shared our fundamental concerns and were receptive to reform.

In the past, there was an atmosphere where politicians were supposed to stay out of the matter, but now ministries and agencies concerned are cooperative, and I sense a shift in the times.

We plan to release a second proposal around this summer. We will formulate concrete measures regarding the strengthening of external intelligence-gathering capabilities and counterintelligence — areas that must be addressed urgently following the enactment of a bill establishing a national intelligence council, which has been submitted to the current special Diet session.

If the government's side establishes an expert panel after the second proposal is submitted, it may be possible to submit a Cabinet-sponsored bill (based on proposals) as early as the ordinary Diet session in 2027.

----

This article is from The Yomiuri Shimbun. Neither Dow Jones Newswires, MarketWatch, Barron's nor The Wall Street Journal were involved in the creation of this content.

YDN-M0000192039-1

data.title: ICBA Opposes OCC’s Conditional Nod For Coinbase National Trust Bank Charter
data.description:
The Office of the Comptroller of the Currency (OCC) granted Coinbase (COIN) a conditional approval for a national trust bank charter, a move that would place the crypto exchange among a small group of five digital-asset firms — including Ripple, Circle (CRCL) — that have received similar tentative sign-offs from the agency. 

If the charter is finalized, Coinbase would be able to expand beyond custody services to offer payment products and other infrastructure under federal supervision, Coinbase’s chief legal officer, Paul Grewal, told CNBC.

Coinbase Eyes Broader US Payments Suite 

During his interview, Grewal said that the approval opens the door for Coinbase to develop a broader range of services in the US, particularly in the area of payments: 

Over the long haul we will be able to explore, with the OCC, offering not just custody products but also other infrastructure products, particularly around payments, that we think will expand and extend crypto payments in all sorts of new and interesting and important directions. 

However, the decision has reignited criticism from traditional banking stakeholders. The Independent Community Bankers of America (ICBA) responded with a letter opposing the OCC’s conditional approval of Coinbase National Trust Co., the subsidiary named in the application. 

ICBA President and CEO Rebeca Romero Rainey called the approval “a grave mistake” that, in the group’s view, would put US consumers at risk. 

The ICBA’s letter alleges the application contains significant shortcomings — including inadequate risk controls, unclear profitability prospects, and unresolved resolution risks — and argues that Coinbase’s filing fails to satisfy requirements set by the National Bank Act and the OCC’s own regulations.

IBCA Demands OCC Rework National Trust Bank Rule 

The trade group warned that the influx of charter applications from non-bank entities suggests firms are seeking the benefits of a federal bank charter without being subject to the full spectrum of bank regulatory safeguards. 

That, the Independent Community Bankers of America alleges, could undermine consumer protection and threaten the broader stability of the financial system. Moreover, the ICBA also aimed at the OCC’s final rule on national trust bank chartering. 

The trade group objects to the OCC’s plan to charter uninsured national trust banks that could carry out non‑fiduciary crypto-related business without being subject to the Bank Holding Company Act or the prudential requirements that apply to FDIC‑insured institutions. 

In its letter, ICBA reiterated calls for the OCC to withdraw the rule or reissue a revised proposal that aligns with the agency’s statutory authority and longstanding legal precedent.

Despite the OCC’s conditional approval, Coinbase’s stock, which trades under the ticker name COIN, was trading at $171 at the time of writing and had seen little to no change compared to Wednesday’s trading session. 

Featured image from OpenArt, chart from TradingView.com
